daily_brief: The Trump-Xi summit in Beijing dominated headlines Thursday, with President Trump arriving accompanied by major US tech CEOs including Elon Musk and Tim Cook [BBC, France 24, Financial Times]. While Trump hailed a 'fantastic relationship,' Xi delivered a pointed warning that Taiwan remains 'the most important issue' and could push the superpowers into conflict if mishandled [France 24, Le Monde]. The summit comes amid a fragile US-Iran ceasefire and ongoing trade tensions, with tariffs on Chinese goods still at 145% [RFI, Globe and Mail]. Meanwhile, Russia launched one of its largest aerial assaults since 2022 against Ukraine, firing over 670 drones and 56 missiles at Kyiv and other cities, killing at least two and wounding dozens just days after a three-day ceasefire collapsed [BBC, France 24, Financial Times, The Hindu]. President Zelensky warned these were 'not the actions of those who believe the war is coming to an end.' In parallel diplomatic activity, BRICS foreign ministers convened in New Delhi to address global divisions over the Iran war and rising energy prices [The Hindu]. Health authorities continue monitoring the hantavirus outbreak from the MV Hondius cruise ship, with France reporting one serious case while officials stress the situation remains contained [RFI, BBC].

top_stories.headline: Trump and Xi Open Beijing Summit as China Warns Taiwan Issue Could Trigger Conflict
bullets: President Trump arrived in Beijing Wednesday evening for a two-day summit with Xi Jinping, the first US presidential visit to China in nearly a decade
Xi warned that Taiwan is 'the most important issue' in US-China relations and that mishandling it could push the nations into 'conflict'
Trump was accompanied by 17 US tech CEOs including Elon Musk (Tesla/SpaceX), Tim Cook (Apple), Jensen Huang (Nvidia), and Larry Fink (BlackRock)

Xi told the executives that Chinese markets will 'open wider' to US and global trade, while a trade truce from October 2025 remains in place amid tariffs of 145% on Chinese goods and 84% Chinese counter-tariffs

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Emphasized pageantry and diplomatic protocol of Trump's arrival

sources.name: France 24
sources.framing_note: Led with Xi's Taiwan warning as the dominant theme

sources.name: RFI English
sources.framing_note: Focused on tariff negotiations and EU concerns about being 'caught in the middle'

sources.name: Financial Times
sources.framing_note: Highlighted Xi's message to tech CEOs about market opening

sources.name: Globe and Mail
sources.framing_note: Contextualized within broader trade war history

top_stories.headline: Russia Launches Massive Aerial Assault on Ukraine as Ceasefire Collapses
bullets: Russia launched over 670 attack drones and 56 missiles against Ukraine overnight, one of its largest strikes since the 2022 invasion began
At least two people were killed and over 40 wounded, with a nine-storey residential building in Kyiv destroyed and 18 apartments lost
The attack marks the third consecutive day of deadly strikes following the expiration of a three-day ceasefire on Monday
President Zelensky urged allies not to remain silent, stating these are 'definitely not the actions of those who believe the war is coming to an end'

sources.name: BBC Europe
sources.framing_note: Detailed rescue operations and infrastructure damage

sources.name: France 24
sources.framing_note: Emphasized scale of attack and Zelensky's diplomatic messaging

sources.name: The Hindu
sources.framing_note: Connected the attack timing to Trump-Xi summit

top_stories.headline: Hantavirus Outbreak From Cruise Ship Contained But Remains Under Close Watch
bullets: Three people have died and nine cases confirmed from the MV Hondius cruise ship outbreak, with two additional suspected cases
A woman is isolating on the remote Pitcairn Islands after contact with an exposed individual but shows no symptoms
A French woman over 65 repatriated from the ship is in intensive care in Paris with severe cardiopulmonary symptoms
WHO chief Tedros said there is 'no sign' of a larger outbreak but warned 'the situation could change'

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Focused on the Pitcairn Islands isolation case and global spread concerns

sources.name: RFI English
sources.framing_note: Emphasized French government reassurances and lessons from COVID-19

top_stories.headline: BRICS Foreign Ministers Meet in New Delhi Amid Iran War Tensions and Energy Price Pressures
bullets: Foreign ministers from BRICS nations began a two-day meeting in New Delhi on Thursday
Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi and Russia's Sergey Lavrov are attending; China sent Ambassador Xu Feihong while Foreign Minister Wang Yi remained in Beijing for Trump's visit
India's Foreign Minister Jaishankar said talks would focus on global challenges and deepening cooperation, noting BRICS should play a 'constructive and stabilising role'
The meeting occurs as the Iran war has disrupted global energy supplies and driven up oil prices

sources.name: The Hindu
sources.framing_note: Detailed diplomatic attendance and framed BRICS as counterweight to Western institutions

top_stories.headline: Israeli Strikes Kill 22 in Southern Lebanon as Ceasefire Violations Continue
bullets: Israeli air strikes killed 22 people including eight children across multiple locations in southern Lebanon
Strikes targeted vehicles on the coastal highway near Beirut and in the city of Sidon, as well as several villages further south
Israeli military said it struck Hezbollah weapons storage facilities and told residents of nine towns to evacuate
UN peacekeeping force UNIFIL expressed concern about increased drone activity near UN positions resulting in explosions around their bases

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Balanced account of casualties and military claims from both sides

additional_stories.headline: South Carolina Supreme Court unanimously overturns Alex Murdaugh's murder convictions, orders new trial due to jury tampering by court clerk
Philippine Senator Ronald dela Rosa, wanted by ICC for crimes against humanity, flees Senate building after gunfire erupts during attempted arrest
Paris Saint-Germain clinch record-extending 14th French Ligue 1 title with 2-0 win over Lens
German court rules Milka chocolate maker Mondelēz deceived consumers by shrinking Alpine Milk bar from 100g to 90g without clear notice
UK economy grew 0.6% in first quarter despite Middle East energy crisis, reducing pressure on Bank of England to cut rates
Human Rights Watch documents execution of 53 civilians by M23 rebels and Rwandan soldiers during occupation of Uvira in eastern DR Congo
French parliamentary report finds Louvre security concerns were 'pushed aside' in favor of prestige projects prior to €88 million jewel heist
Internal displacements caused by conflict reached record 32.3 million in 2025, surpassing disaster-driven displacement for first time
